{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: notes
Each entry gives the derivative of the **column quantity**
with respect to the **row quantity**;
that is, the entry in row $i$ and column $j$ is
$\derivf{(\text{col } j)}{(\text{row } i)}$.

**Dimensions** of each entry depend on the types of the row and column quantities:

- **Scalar/scalar** (upper-left $3 \times 3$ block):
  row and column are both scalars ($\prob$, $\odds$, or $\logodds$);
  derivative is a scalar.
- **Gradient** (lower-left $2 \times 3$ block):
  row is a $p$-vector ($\vx$ or $\vb$), column is a scalar;
  derivative is a $p$-vector (gradient).
- **Jacobian** (lower-right $2 \times 2$ block):
  both row and column are $p$-vectors;
  derivative is a $p \times p$ matrix.
- **Undefined** (upper-right $3 \times 2$ block):
  row is a scalar, column is $\vx$ or $\vb$.
  Since $\vx$ and $\vb$ are model inputs rather than functions of
  $\prob$, $\odds$, or $\logodds$,
  these derivatives are not defined.
:::

:::{#tbl-logistic-deriv-matrix}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_eq-logistic-deriv-matrix.qmd >}}

Matrix of logistic regression derivatives
($p$ = number of predictors,
$\logodds \eqdef \dprod{\vx}{\vb}$,
$\odds \eqdef \expf{\logodds}$,
$\prob \eqdef \odds / (1 + \odds)$).
Each entry is the derivative of the **column quantity**
with respect to the **row quantity**.
Entries marked "undef" are not defined
because the column quantity is a model input,
not a function of the row quantity.
:::
